subject: Allstate Claim 0722264736 — Shaun Ali — Adjuster Runaround + Luis Esteves Escalation Template
carrier: Allstate Vehicle and Property Insurance Company
forwardedBy: Benjamin Salgado: 'They keep giving me the run around. The old claim needs to be supplemented. I legit recorded the adjuster saying he acknowledged that the damage is from the 2023 storm. Any suggestions on how to escalate this because this is ridiculous'
carrierResponse: At this time, the most recent claim denial will need to be disputed in order to address the damages your contractor has identified. According to our records, the damages your contractor wants to claim were not present at the time of loss for this claim. ... Before I contacted your current contractor and as soon as your claim was assigned to me, I spoke with the previous adjuster handling your claim. I also consulted with leadership to confirm the proper way to handle this situation. ... At this point, you will need to dispute the denial under the new claim.
homeownerCounter: Two adjusters have acknowledged the presence of damage to my roof, including one who physically inspected the property. Additionally, an inspector hired through Allstate and my current contractor have both documented and confirmed the damage as well. Initially, I was advised to file a new claim, which I did in good faith based on the guidance provided to me. When the new adjuster conducted their inspection, they confirmed that the damage was consistent with and caused by the 2023 storm event, and advised that the correct course of action would be to supplement the original claim. Following that direction, my contractor submitted a request to supplement the original claim. However, I was then told to redirect the request to the newer claim instead. This back-and-forth has continued... In fact, I have an audio recording of the adjuster clearly stating that the damages are from the prior storm. It truly does not get more definitive than that.
rooferEscalationTemplate: Dear Mr. Cherry, I am writing to formally dispute the denial of my claim and request that this matter be escalated to a Claims Supervisor for review. During the most recent inspection, the field adjuster acknowledged that the roof damage present on my property is consistent with and caused by the 2023 storm event. Based on that assessment, I was advised that the appropriate course of action would be to supplement the original claim. My contractor subsequently submitted documentation supporting the damage and requesting that the claim be supplemented. I am now being told the damage was 'not present at the time of loss,' which directly contradicts the findings communicated during the inspection. For clarity, I have documentation and a recorded conversation with the adjuster acknowledging the damage is consistent with the 2023 storm. Given this information, the continued denial raises concerns regarding a failure to properly investigate the claim and reconcile the findings between inspections. At this time, I am requesting: Escalation of this claim to a Claims Supervisor or Manager; A reinspection by an Allstate staff adjuster or field supervisor, not a third-party inspector; The specific policy language being relied upon to support the denial. If the claim continues to be denied, please also provide a copy of the claim file documentation used to support that determination. I would prefer to resolve this matter directly with Allstate; however, if necessary, I am prepared to escalate the issue further, including filing a complaint with the Virginia State Corporation Commission and getting my lawyer involved.
rooferTacticNotes: Luis Esteves (Roof-ER) tips: (1) formally requesting supervisor/manager review — Insurance companies treat supervisor escalations differently because they create internal documentation. (2) Re-inspect by Allstate staff adjuster or field supervisor, NOT a third-party inspector. (3) Demand specific policy language being relied upon. (4) Threaten legal action / escalate to Virginia State Corporation Commission.
keyDenialLanguage: the damages your contractor wants to claim were not present at the time of loss for this claim
you will need to dispute the denial under the new claim
spoke with the previous adjuster handling your claim. I also consulted with leadership
carrierTactic: DUAL-CLAIM SHELL GAME — When supplement is requested under original claim, redirect to new claim. When supplement is requested under new claim, redirect back to original. Adjuster acknowledges damage verbally but denies in writing. Cites internal leadership consultation as cover.
rooferTactic: RECORDED VERBAL ACKNOWLEDGMENT is the strongest evidence — get adjuster on tape acknowledging damage source, then point to the written denial as contradiction. Plus the formal escalation template covers all bases: supervisor escalation, internal-staff inspection, policy language demand, regulatory complaint threat.
patentMapping: Allstate's automated claim routing (US11798095, US11348134) creates the dual-claim ambiguity — different AI confidence scores per claim number create the runaround. Patent rules say 'route to highest-confidence outcome' but when multiple claims exist for same property, the system can't reconcile.
lessonForAnalyzer: This Luis Esteves escalation template is the GOLD STANDARD for Allstate denials. Counter-letter generator should produce ALL FOUR demands: (1) supervisor escalation, (2) staff adjuster reinspection (not 3rd-party), (3) specific policy language citation, (4) claim file documentation. Plus regulatory complaint threat (Virginia SCC or state equivalent).
